rulings.Case Title: PAUL A. VANDER WAERDT VS. CHARLES M. COLLINS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn To Strike Memo Of Costs/Alt To Tax Costs
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, May 4, 2009, line 3, PLAINTIFF PAUL VANDER WAERDT'S Motion To Strike Memorandum Of Costs, in the alernative to Tax Costs. Motion is granted, in part. Defendant is the prevailing party. CCP section 1032. $1,596.71 court call costs are stricken, and out of state commission costs incurred for taking plaintiff's deposition are stricken as not reasonably necessary. =(302/CWW/JW)

rulings.Case Title: WALTER COX et al VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (BP) AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS OR STAY OR DISMISS
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, May 4, 2009, line 4, DEFENDANT JONERIC PRODUCTS, INC.'S MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS OR STAY OR DISMISS. Off Calendar, per dismissal. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: RAIN STOGDEN VS. ARTHUR ACEVEDO et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Tax Costs
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, May 4, 2009, line 5, PLAINTIFF RAIN STOGDEN'S Motion To Tax Costs. Motion is denied. CRC section 3.1700 provides service and filing of a memorandum of costs within fifteen days after the date of mailing of the notice of entry of judgment or dismissal by the clerk. Costs are awarded. =(302/CWW/JW)

rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, May 4, 2009, line 6, PLAINTIFF CLAYTON HARMSTON'S Motion Objecting to evidence and Moving to Strike Evidence Submitted By Defendants In Support Of Defendants' Motion For Summary Judgment. Motion to strike is denied. =(302/CWW/BP)

rulings.Case Title: CLAYTON HARMSTON et al VS. CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, May 4, 2009, line 6, DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO, and, HEATHER FONG'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT/OR IN THE ALTERNATIVE SUMMARY ADJUDICATION. Motion for Summary Adjudication is granted for discrimination and intentional infliction of emotional distress claims. Motion is denied as to Retaliation claims for Parry, Harmston, and Lewis. Motion is granted as to Retaliation claims for Cohen and Hurley. =(302/CWW/BP)

rulings.Case Title: JONATHAN SUN VS. INDEPENDENT TELEVISON SERVICE INC A CORPORATION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, May 4, 2009, line 15, DEFENDANT INDEPENDENT TELEVISION SERVICE, INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION. Motion for summary adjudication is granted. As to the quantum meruit cause of action, plaintiff fails to create a triable issue of fact that plaintiff's services benefitted defendant. As to the cause of action for intentional interference with prospective business advantage, plaintiff fails to create a triable issue of fact that defendant's conduct was wrongful by some legal measure other than the fact of interference itself, or that defendant was an outsider that interfered with a contractual relationship that plaintiff had with Jetson/LNTV. =(302/CWW/VC)

rulings.Case Title: CARLOS ARNOLDO LOPEZ et al VS. JOSE NUILA et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Plaintiffs Order To Show Cause Re Liens
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, May 4, 2009, line 8, Plaintiffs' Order to Show Cause regarding Liens (per ex parte Order of april 10, 2009). Application granted pursuant to stipulation. San Francisco General Hospital Medical Group and Provider Recovery Network lien is stricken. Lien of CCSF against Carlos Andres Lopez is reduced to $450. Lien of CCSF against Marcos Daniel Lopez is reduced to $550. =(302/CWW/PB)

rulings.Case Title: YVONNE L. MAFFEY VS. JOHN CHIANG
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion Andmotion For Judgment On The Pleadings
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, May 4, 2009, line 11, DEFENDANT JOHN CHIANG'S Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings. Motion is granted. The issue of whether plaintiff was entitled to the subject escheated funds was previously adjudicated by the Alameda Superior Court in case number RP03-131 453. Plaintiff is barred from relitigating that issue in this action. No other issue is before the Court. =(302/CWW/JW)

rulings.Case Title: STEVEN DALE PERCELLE VS. SWANSON, MCNAMARA & HALLER et al (Provide Access)
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, May 4, 2009, line 13, DEFENDANT SWANSON, MCNAMARA & HALLER, MARY MCNAMARA, and, AUGUST GUGELMANN'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. Demurrer is sustained and motion to strike is granted, both with ten days leave to amend. Plaintiff fails to plead actual innocence, identify his injury, show a proximate causal connection, or allege facts sufficient to support punitive damages. =(302/CWW/JW)
